Daily HHS Accessibility Report — 2026-05-16#

Run ID: run-2026-05-16-8283aa4b59 · Status: partial · Source data: 2026-05-14 · Generated: 2026-05-16T09:50:21.649Z

View HTML/CSS/JS Code Quality report for this date →

About These Reports#

U.S. federal government website traffic data is publicly available at analytics.usa.gov, powered by the Digital Analytics Program (DAP) — a shared analytics service that tracks traffic across hundreds of participating federal agencies and publishes the data openly. This report focuses on the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) and its sub-agencies, using HHS-specific traffic data from analytics.usa.gov/health-human-services.

This report measures the quality and accessibility of the top 100 most-visited HHS URLs as reported by analytics.usa.gov. Each day, Lighthouse scans are run against these URLs to measure:

  • Performance - How fast pages load for users (scores 0-100, higher is better)
  • Accessibility - How well pages work for users with disabilities, following WCAG guidelines (scores 0-100, higher is better)
  • Best Practices - Whether pages follow modern web development standards (scores 0-100, higher is better)
  • SEO - How well pages are optimized for search engines (scores 0-100, higher is better)

Accessibility findings come from axe-core, the industry-standard accessibility testing engine embedded in Lighthouse. The axe findings surface specific WCAG violations such as missing alternative text, insufficient color contrast, and missing form labels that make government websites harder to use for people with disabilities.

Traffic data reflects daily visitor counts from analytics.usa.gov for HHS properties. URLs are ranked by page load count, ensuring the most-used HHS pages are prioritized for quality measurement.

Accessibility Trend Narrative#

Over the past 29 days of data (2026-04-16 to 2026-05-15), government website accessibility scores have remained stable (+0.34 points, from 92.13 to 92.47). Performance scores have declined (-0.67 points, from 53.99 to 53.32).

Today's aggregate accessibility score of 89.74 reflects the mean Lighthouse accessibility score across 49 successfully scanned government URLs. A score above 90 indicates generally strong compliance with WCAG automated checks, though manual testing is always recommended to fully assess accessibility.

Common Accessibility Issues (Top 10)#

The following axe-core rules were most frequently violated across scanned URLs today. These patterns indicate systemic accessibility barriers present across multiple government websites.

Total axe findings today: 89 across 77 scanned URLs.

Human impact narratives for top issues

The following narratives describe the real-world impact of each accessibility barrier on people with disabilities. These barriers are not abstract technical failures; they prevent citizens from independently accessing government services.

landmark-one-main: Primary Content Identification

Without a main landmark, screen reader users lose their most efficient way to skip to the primary content of a government page. Every page visit requires tabbing through the entire navigation structure to reach the actual content, a significant time and effort burden that accumulates across repeated visits to government websites for citizens managing ongoing benefit cases or legal matters.

Affected groups:

  • People who are blind using screen readers
  • People who are deaf and rely on visual-to-text tools
  • People with motor disabilities using keyboard navigation

tabindex: Focus Order Predictability

Positive tabindex values create unpredictable focus sequences that jump around the page in ways that differ from the visual reading order. For keyboard users and screen reader users on government websites, an erratic tab order makes it difficult to reliably navigate forms and interactive content, increasing errors and the time needed to complete tasks, and creating particular frustration for users with cognitive disabilities who need a predictable, logical interaction flow.

Affected groups:

  • People who are blind using screen readers
  • People who are deaf using keyboard-only navigation
  • People with motor disabilities using keyboard or switch access

target-size: Digital Motor Access

Small touch targets act as a digital gatekeeper, excluding individuals with tremors, arthritis, or limited dexterity from accessing essential services independently. These technical failures transform a routine interaction into a source of failure, stripping away the autonomy of citizens who require a frictionless, accessible interface to participate in digital life. The approximately 58 million Americans with ambulatory or self-care disabilities are disproportionately impacted by inadequate touch target sizing on government mobile websites.

Affected groups:

  • People with Parkinson's disease, arthritis, or hand tremors
  • Older adults with reduced fine motor control
  • People with motor disabilities using alternative pointing devices
  • People in situational impairment contexts (e.g., commuting, holding a child)

link-name: Link Purpose Clarity

Links without accessible names are completely useless to screen reader users who navigate government pages by jumping between links. An unnamed link could lead anywhere, and activating it unknowingly could trigger downloads, open unexpected pages, or initiate unintended processes. On government websites, unnamed links undermine the informed consent principle by preventing citizens from knowing where a link will take them.

Affected groups:

  • People who are blind using screen readers
  • People who are deaf and rely on visual-to-text tools
  • People with motor disabilities using keyboard navigation

color-contrast: Visual Information Access

Low contrast text is one of the most pervasive barriers on government websites, rendering critical information invisible to the approximately 26 million Americans with low vision or color blindness. When agency announcements, form instructions, error messages, or legal notices are displayed in insufficient contrast, affected citizens are denied equal access to the information they need to exercise their rights and access public services.

Affected groups:

  • People with low vision including age-related vision loss
  • People who are color blind (approximately 8% of men, 0.5% of women)

skip-link: Skip Link Functional Effectiveness

A skip link that points to a non-existent or non-focusable target provides no actual benefit and may confuse keyboard users who activate it and experience no apparent result. For screen reader and keyboard users on government websites with extensive navigation, a broken skip link means they must navigate through the entire repeated header every time, negating the accessibility feature entirely and creating a cruel illusion of accessibility that does not function.

Affected groups:

  • People who are blind using screen readers
  • People who are deaf using keyboard-only navigation
  • People with motor disabilities using keyboard navigation

heading-order: Document Structure Navigation

Screen reader users navigate complex government websites primarily through heading structure, using headings as a table of contents to jump between sections. Skipped heading levels break the logical document outline, causing confusion about the hierarchy of information and forcing users to re-read sections to understand the relationship between topics, adding significant time and effort to information-gathering tasks.

Affected groups:

  • People who are blind using screen readers
  • People who are deaf and rely on visual-to-text tools
  • People with motor disabilities using keyboard navigation

link-in-text-block: Color-Independent Link Identification

When links are distinguished only by color, people with color vision deficiency cannot identify them as links and may miss critical references to additional information, forms, or resources. On government websites, overlooked links in informational content can mean missing essential instructions, deadlines, or procedures that affect a citizen's ability to access services or comply with requirements.

Affected groups:

  • People with low vision who may not perceive color differences
  • People who are color blind (approximately 8% of men, 0.5% of women)

image-alt: Visual Content Access

Images without alternative text are completely invisible to blind users and to search engines. On government websites, informational images, diagrams, infographics, and document screenshots without alt text deprive blind citizens of the same information available to sighted visitors. Critical details like emergency instructions, benefit eligibility charts, or form procedure diagrams can be entirely inaccessible, putting these citizens at a disadvantage in accessing government services and information.

Affected groups:

  • People who are blind using screen readers
  • People who are deaf and rely on visual-to-text tools

label: Form Field Identification

Unlabeled form fields are the most common barrier preventing blind and low-vision citizens from completing government forms. When input fields lack programmatic labels, screen reader users cannot determine what information is being requested, making it impossible to apply for benefits, file taxes, register to vote, or access virtually any government digital service that requires form input. This single failure can block an entire service interaction.

Affected groups:

  • People who are blind using screen readers
  • People with low vision relying on assistive technology
  • People who are deaf using keyboard navigation
  • People with motor disabilities using voice control
Disability icon key
Without Vision — People who are blind or have no functional vision (1.0% of U.S. population — ~3,400,000 Americans)
Limited Vision — People with low vision who need magnification or high contrast (2.4% of U.S. population — ~8,100,000 Americans)
Without Perception of Color — People who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness) (4.3% of U.S. population — ~14,500,000 Americans)
Without Hearing — People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content (0.3% of U.S. population — ~1,100,000 Americans)
Limited Hearing — People with hearing loss who may struggle with audio without accommodations (3.5% of U.S. population — ~11,900,000 Americans)
Without Speech — People who cannot use speech or voice-based input effectively (0.5% of U.S. population — ~1,700,000 Americans)
Limited Manipulation — People with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity (2.2% of U.S. population — ~7,600,000 Americans)
Limited Reach and Strength — People with limited reach, strength, or stamina (5.8% of U.S. population — ~19,600,000 Americans)
Limited Language, Cognitive, and Learning Abilities — People with cognitive, learning, or language differences (4.7% of U.S. population — ~15,900,000 Americans)

These icons show which groups of people with disabilities are excluded by each accessibility barrier. Where page view data is available, each icon shows an estimated number of people potentially excluded (page loads for affected URLs × disability prevalence rate from U.S. Census ACS 2023). Hover over or focus an icon to see the full estimate and methodology. Icons follow the Section 508 Functional Performance Criteria and the equivalent EU EN 301 549 v3.2.1 Table B.2 categories.

Prevalence data: U.S. Census Bureau, American Community Survey (ACS) 2023 1-Year Estimates, Table B18101; supplemental data from CDC, NIDCD, AFB, and NIH/NEI. U.S. population base: ~336,500,000 (2023 estimate). Reviewed annually.

See the Section 508 WCAG to FPC mapping for additional detail on how accessibility requirements map to functional needs.

Usability Heuristics Summary#

The most common accessibility violations map to Nielsen Norman Group's 10 usability heuristics. This table shows which heuristics are most affected by today's top axe-core violations, helping to identify where systemic usability barriers exist.

NN/g usability heuristics affected by today's top accessibility violations
# Heuristic Issue patterns URL violations Related axe rules
6 Recognition rather than recall 5 41 landmark-one-main, link-name, color-contrast, heading-order, label
8 Aesthetic and minimalist design 5 37 landmark-one-main, color-contrast, link-in-text-block, image-alt, label
10 Help and documentation 5 37 landmark-one-main, color-contrast, link-in-text-block, image-alt, label
4 Consistency and standards 4 33 landmark-one-main, link-name, heading-order, label
1 Visibility of system status 4 32 landmark-one-main, color-contrast, link-in-text-block, label
9 Help users recognize, diagnose, and recover from errors 4 32 landmark-one-main, color-contrast, link-in-text-block, label
5 Error prevention 3 24 landmark-one-main, link-in-text-block, label
7 Flexibility and efficiency of use 3 20 link-name, skip-link, heading-order

Americans Excluded by Disability Today#

Based on 2,227,227 page loads across successfully scanned government URLs and U.S. disability prevalence rates from the U.S. Census Bureau, American Community Survey (ACS) 2023 1-Year Estimates, Table B18101 (2023), an estimated 73,732 Americans encountered an accessibility barrier on a government website today.

Each row shows the number of people in a disability group who visited a page with at least one accessibility issue that affects their group. Prevalence rates and U.S. population estimates are derived from the American Community Survey (ACS) and supplemental sources. These figures are rough estimates intended to illustrate the scale of accessibility barriers.

Estimated Americans excluded today by disability category (2023 Census data)
Icon Disability Group (Section 508 FPC) U.S. Prevalence U.S. Population Affected Page Loads with Barriers Est. Americans Excluded Today
Limited Manipulation 2.2% ~7,600,000 1,850,000 40,736
Without Vision 1.0% ~3,400,000 1,820,000 18,298
Without Hearing 0.3% ~1,100,000 1,820,000 5,489
Without Perception of Color 4.3% ~14,500,000 100,000 4,422
Limited Vision 2.4% ~8,100,000 110,000 2,758
Limited Reach and Strength 5.8% ~19,600,000 34,186 1,983
Limited Language, Cognitive, and Learning Abilities 4.7% ~15,900,000 964 45

Sources: U.S. Census Bureau, American Community Survey (ACS) 2023 1-Year Estimates, Table B18101; disability prevalence from ACS 2023 and supplemental CDC/NIDCD/NIH data. Reviewed annually.

Performance Impact on Americans#

Google defines a good Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) as under 2.5 seconds and recommends pages under 1.6 MB. The figures below estimate how much extra time Americans spend waiting, and how much extra data is transferred, because government websites fall short of these benchmarks. Calculations are based on 38 successfully scanned URLs with Lighthouse timing data.

Estimated performance impact vs. Google benchmarks for government URLs scanned on May 16, 2026
Metric Estimated total (May 16, 2026) Notes
Extra time waiting (vs 2.5s LCP benchmark) 8 months, 19 daysExtra time is calculated as: for each scanned URL, max(0, actual LCP − 2.5s) × page loads. Total: 22,728,032 seconds. 22,728,032 seconds
Extra data transferred (vs 1.6 MB page weight benchmark) ~4 TB (168 copies of Wikipedia)Extra data is calculated as: for each scanned URL, max(0, actual page weight − 1.6 MB) × page loads. Across 38 URLs with weight data

LCP and page weight are measured by Lighthouse. Wikipedia copy count uses a size of 24.05 GB per Wikipedia:Size of Wikipedia. These are rough estimates based on a sample of the top government URLs by traffic. Hover or focus the estimated totals for calculation details.

Content Density (Words per Megabyte)#

Words-per-Megabyte (WpM) measures how efficiently a page delivers readable content relative to its total download size. A low ratio indicates Digital Bloat—the page transfers far more data than the text it contains. Pages below 200 WpM are flagged for optimization review.

  • URLs with content metrics: 20
  • Average word count: 220
  • Average Words/MB: 140
  • URLs below 200 WpM threshold: 14
Content density for top URLs scanned on May 16, 2026 (up to 10 shown)
URL Word Count Words/MB

https://gudid.fda.gov 213 199
https://nationaldppcsc.cdc.gov 4 1
https://ncsacw.acf.gov 145 20

Word counts are extracted from the page’s main article content using Mozilla Readability, which strips navigation, sidebars, and ads. Page weight is the total resource size measured by Lighthouse. Pages below 200 WpM may impose unnecessary data costs on users on metered connections.

Accessibility Statements (M-24-08)#

OMB Memorandum M-24-08 requires each federal agency to publish a digital accessibility statement that includes contact information for accessibility issues, known limitations, and a link to the agency Section 508 program page.

15 of 49 domains (31%) checked have a detectable accessibility statement at a standard URL path.

Required Federal Links (M-17-06)#

OMB Memorandum M-17-06 and the 21st Century IDEA Act require federal agency public websites to provide links to a Privacy Policy, Contact page, and FOIA page. These were among the compliance criteria benchmarked by the performance.gov federal website performance initiative (no longer active, but the underlying requirements remain in force).

16 of 49 domains (33%) have all three required link types detectable at standard URL paths.

Section 508: Legal Requirements vs. Best Practices#

Section 508 of the Rehabilitation Act was enacted in 1998 and requires U.S. federal agencies to make their electronic and information technology accessible to people with disabilities. The technical standards were significantly updated through the ICT Accessibility Standards refresh, which became effective in January 2018 — the same year Apple celebrated the 10th anniversary of the iPhone. That refresh incorporated WCAG 2.0 Level AA (published in 2008) as the baseline for web content.

The web has changed dramatically since then. What agencies are legally required to meet today is built on a standard from 2008 — the same era as the original iPhone. Agencies should treat WCAG 2.0 AA as the floor, not the ceiling.

Legal Requirement

WCAG 2.0 Level AA (published 2008)

Referenced by the Section 508 ICT Standards refresh (effective 2018). Agencies must meet these 38 success criteria to satisfy their Section 508 obligations for web content.

  • Text alternatives for non-text content
  • Captions and audio descriptions for multimedia
  • Keyboard accessibility and focus management
  • Minimum color contrast (4.5:1 for normal text)
  • No seizure-inducing content
  • Consistent navigation and labeling
  • Error identification and correction

Section 508 web content requirements (E205)

Best Practices

WCAG 2.1 and 2.2 Level AA (published 2018 and 2023)

These later versions add criteria that reflect how the web actually works today — mobile devices, cognitive accessibility, and modern authentication patterns — and are strongly recommended for any agency aiming to serve all Americans. The US Federal Government is one of the last to use the legacy WCAG 2.0 AA Standard, as it was developed ~2 decades ago.

  • WCAG 2.1 (2018): 17 new criteria for mobile, low vision, and cognitive accessibility
  • WCAG 2.2 (2023): 9 more criteria for authentication and cognitive tasks, removing the outdated "Parsing" criterion
  • W3C now recommends WCAG 2.2 as the current normative standard for new and updated content

What is new in WCAG 2.2 · WCAG 2.2 specification

The scores and findings in this report are based on automated checks aligned with WCAG 2.x rules surfaced by axe-core. Passing automated checks is necessary but not sufficient: manual testing with assistive technologies is required to verify true conformance and to catch barriers that automation cannot detect.

Take Action#

Today's scan identified 89 accessibility barriers across the most-visited U.S. government websites, affecting an estimated 73,732 Americans with disabilities. Here is how you can help.

  • Read the federal accessibility report. The 2025 Governmentwide Section 508 Assessment from the GSA Administrator details the state of federal accessibility compliance and the steps agencies are taking to improve.
  • Submit URLs you care about. Want specific government pages included in future scans? Submit them to the Open Scans project to help broaden coverage.
  • Test with free tools. Do your own automated and manual accessibility testing with Accessibility Insights and Google Lighthouse. Automated tools catch a significant portion of WCAG failures quickly; manual review with assistive technologies catches the rest.
  • Adopt and update the U.S. Web Design System (USWDS). The USWDS is already a strong foundation for accessible, consistent federal websites, but many agencies have not adopted it or are running outdated versions. Encourage your agency to adopt or upgrade so every American benefits from its accessibility work.
  • Hire people with disabilities. People with disabilities are the ultimate experts on digital barriers. Bringing them into design, engineering, and testing teams — not just as consultants but as full-time employees — leads to more accessible products and services for everyone.

Accessibility Findings

https://cdc.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 91

Axe findings: 3

Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name) HHS-3e7fec37

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~9KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~9,225 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 922,466 affected page loads) ~2KPeople who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~2,767 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 922,466 affected page loads) ~20KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~20,294 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 922,466 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 HHS-2428d6f9

Element path: nav.mobile-navbar > div.mobile-navbar_top > div.d-flex > a#cdc-mobile-search-toggle

Snippet:

<a id="cdc-mobile-search-toggle" class="nav-link" href="#" aria-controls="cdc-mobile-search-form">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Element is in tab order and does not have accessible text
  • Fix any of the following:
  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
Skip links are not focusable. (rule: skip-link) HHS-b3698d5b

Including a skip link can help users skip to the main content to save time. Learn more about skip links.

Disabilities affected: ~9KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~9,225 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 922,466 affected page loads) ~2KPeople who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~2,767 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 922,466 affected page loads) ~20KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~20,294 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 922,466 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 HHS-d7fc04fe

Element path: body.cdc-post-type--cdc-homepage > div#skipmenu > a.skippy

Snippet:

<a class="skippy visually-hidden-focusable" href="#headerSearch" tabindex="2">

Label: Skip to search

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • No skip link target
Some elements have a `[tabindex]` value greater than 0 (rule: tabindex) HHS-6eb16f7d

A value greater than 0 implies an explicit navigation ordering. Although technically valid, this often creates frustrating experiences for users who rely on assistive technologies. Learn more about the `tabindex` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~9KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~9,225 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 922,466 affected page loads) ~2KPeople who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~2,767 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 922,466 affected page loads) ~20KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~20,294 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 922,466 affected page loads)

Affected elements (3):

Element 1 HHS-2b0faadb

Element path: body.cdc-post-type--cdc-homepage > div#skipmenu > a.skippy

Snippet:

<a class="skippy visually-hidden-focusable" href="#content" tabindex="1">

Label: Skip to site content

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has a tabindex greater than 0

Element 2 HHS-2b0faadb

Element path: body.cdc-post-type--cdc-homepage > div#skipmenu > a.skippy

Snippet:

<a class="skippy visually-hidden-focusable" href="#headerSearch" tabindex="2">

Label: Skip to search

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has a tabindex greater than 0

Element 3 HHS-3994b993

Element path: div#hero-carousel > div.carousel-indicators > div.carousel-indicators-wrapper > button#animation-toggle

Snippet:

<button id="animation-toggle" aria-pressed="true" tabindex="3">

Label: Pause carousel

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has a tabindex greater than 0

Accessibility Findings

https://cms.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 90

Axe findings: 2

`[role]`s are not contained by their required parent element (rule: aria-required-parent) HHS-f9019603

Some ARIA child roles must be contained by specific parent roles to properly perform their intended accessibility functions. Learn more about ARIA roles and required parent element.

Disabilities affected: ~4KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~4,850 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 484,972 affected page loads) ~1KPeople who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~1,455 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 484,972 affected page loads) ~10KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Affected elements (5):

Element 1 HHS-7d6746f3

Element path: div.field__item > div.paragraph > div.ds-u-lg-display--none > button#trigger-tabs-869237318

Snippet:

<button type="button" id="trigger-tabs-869237318" aria-expanded="false" class="responsive-tabs__tab-button--mobile ds-u-display--flex" role="tab" aria-controls="tabs-869237318">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Required ARIA parent role not present: tablist

Element 2 HHS-bd2c8e82

Element path: div.field__item > div.paragraph > div.ds-u-lg-display--none > button#trigger-tabs-660533254

Snippet:

<button type="button" id="trigger-tabs-660533254" aria-expanded="false" class="responsive-tabs__tab-button--mobile ds-u-display--flex" role="tab" aria-controls="tabs-660533254">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Required ARIA parent role not present: tablist

Element 3 HHS-d474dadc

Element path: div.field__item > div.paragraph > div.ds-u-lg-display--none > button#trigger-tabs-1673532279

Snippet:

<button type="button" id="trigger-tabs-1673532279" aria-expanded="false" class="responsive-tabs__tab-button--mobile ds-u-display--flex" role="tab" aria-controls="tabs-1673532279">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Required ARIA parent role not present: tablist

Element 4 HHS-ac9cc63a

Element path: div.field__item > div.paragraph > div.ds-u-lg-display--none > button#trigger-tabs-1129973070

Snippet:

<button type="button" id="trigger-tabs-1129973070" aria-expanded="false" class="responsive-tabs__tab-button--mobile ds-u-display--flex" role="tab" aria-controls="tabs-1129973070">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Required ARIA parent role not present: tablist

Element 5 HHS-4ab135ad

Element path: div.field__item > div.paragraph > div.ds-u-lg-display--none > button#trigger-tabs-1302461697

Snippet:

<button type="button" id="trigger-tabs-1302461697" aria-expanded="false" class="responsive-tabs__tab-button--mobile ds-u-display--flex" role="tab" aria-controls="tabs-1302461697">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Required ARIA parent role not present: tablist
Buttons do not have an accessible name (rule: button-name) HHS-756f8154

When a button doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it as "button", making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. Learn how to make buttons more accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~4KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~4,850 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 484,972 affected page loads) ~1KPeople who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~1,455 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 484,972 affected page loads)

Affected elements (5):

Element 1 HHS-33afc660

Element path: div.field__item > div.paragraph > div.ds-u-lg-display--none > button#trigger-tabs-869237318

Snippet:

<button type="button" id="trigger-tabs-869237318" aria-expanded="false" class="responsive-tabs__tab-button--mobile ds-u-display--flex" role="tab" aria-controls="tabs-869237318">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have inner text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

Accessibility Findings

https://fda.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 95

Axe findings: 2

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order) HHS-ecc5de53

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Disabilities affected: ~3KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~3,518 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 351,763 affected page loads) ~1KPeople who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~1,055 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 351,763 affected page loads) ~7KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~7,739 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 351,763 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 HHS-b98a7353

Element path: div.fda-ft-container > div.fda-ft-heading > div.fda-ft-heading-text > h3.fda-ft-heading-title

Snippet:

<h3 class="fda-ft-heading-title">

Label: Featured Topics

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Heading order invalid
Some elements have a `[tabindex]` value greater than 0 (rule: tabindex) HHS-4d2e97e5

A value greater than 0 implies an explicit navigation ordering. Although technically valid, this often creates frustrating experiences for users who rely on assistive technologies. Learn more about the `tabindex` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~3KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~3,518 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 351,763 affected page loads) ~1KPeople who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~1,055 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 351,763 affected page loads) ~7KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~7,739 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 351,763 affected page loads)

Affected elements (3):

Element 1 HHS-16ddb43c

Element path: div#quicklinks > ul.list-inline > li > a.btn

Snippet:

<a href="#main-content" class="btn btn-default margin-top-3" tabindex="1" data-once="download-role-button lang-fix-multilingual">

Label: Skip to main content

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has a tabindex greater than 0

Element 2 HHS-16ddb43c

Element path: div#quicklinks > ul.list-inline > li > a.btn

Snippet:

<a href="#search-form" class="btn btn-default margin-top-3" tabindex="1" data-once="download-role-button lang-fix-multilingual">

Label: Skip to FDA Search

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has a tabindex greater than 0

Element 3 HHS-16ddb43c

Element path: div#quicklinks > ul.list-inline > li > a.btn

Snippet:

<a href="#footer" class="btn btn-default margin-top-3" tabindex="1" data-once="download-role-button lang-fix-multilingual">

Label: Skip to footer links

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has a tabindex greater than 0

Download full axe findings JSON | Download full axe findings CSV

Accessibility Findings

https://nih.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 96

Axe findings: 1

Links rely on color to be distinguishable. (rule: link-in-text-block) HHS-3c20b2e6

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Link text that is discernible improves the experience for users with low vision. Learn how to make links distinguishable.

Disabilities affected: ~2KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~2,163 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 90,108 affected page loads) ~3KPeople who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Estimated ~3,875 people potentially excluded (4.3% prevalence × 90,108 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 HHS-67550629

Element path: div.usa-identifier__container > div.usa-identifier__identity > p.usa-identifier__identity-disclaimer > a.ext

Snippet:

<a href="http://www.hhs.gov/" class="ext" data-extlink="" target="_blank" rel="noopener nofollow noreferrer">

Label: Department of Health and Human Services

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 1.31:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #dfe1e2, surrounding text: #ffffff)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text

Accessibility Findings

https://wwwnc.cdc.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 91

Axe findings: 3

Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name) HHS-a1e74b24

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~331People who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~331 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 33,110 affected page loads) ~99People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Element 1 HHS-39eea2eb

Element path: nav.mobile-navbar > div.mobile-navbar_top > div.d-flex > a#cdc-mobile-search-toggle

  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute

Accessibility Findings

https://acf.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 97

Axe findings: 1

Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size) HHS-94089b69

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Disabilities affected: ~472People with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~472 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 21,476 affected page loads) ~1KPeople with limited reach, strength, or stamina. Estimated ~1,246 people potentially excluded (5.8% prevalence × 21,476 affected page loads)

Affected elements (7):

Element 1 HHS-c0b3ae90

Element path: li > ul > li > a.external

Snippet:

<a href="https://www.hhs.gov" class="external" target="_blank">

Label: HHS.gov

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size because it is partially obscured (smallest space is 59.4px by 19.2px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 15.8px instead of at least 24px.

Element 2 HHS-c0b3ae90

Element path: li > ul > li > a.external

Snippet:

<a href="https://www.grants.gov" class="external" target="_blank">

Label: Grants.gov

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size because it is partially obscured (smallest space is 70.2px by 19.2px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 15.8px instead of at least 24px.

Element 3 HHS-c0b3ae90

Element path: li > ul > li > a.external

Snippet:

<a href="https://www.usajobs.gov/" class="external" target="_blank">

Label: USAJobs.gov

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size because it is partially obscured (smallest space is 81.8px by 19.2px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 16px instead of at least 24px.

Element 4 HHS-c0b3ae90

Element path: li > ul > li > a.external

Snippet:

<a href="https://oig.hhs.gov/fraud/report-fraud/" class="external" target="_blank">

Label: Report Fraud

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size because it is partially obscured (smallest space is 81.7px by 19.2px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 19.2px instead of at least 24px.

Element 5 HHS-4e6001ad

Element path: li > ul > li > a

Snippet:

<a href="/accessibility">

Label: Accessibility

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size because it is partially obscured (smallest space is 61.2px by 19.2px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 13.4px instead of at least 24px.

Element 6 HHS-4e6001ad

Element path: li > ul > li > a

Snippet:

<a href="/about/acf-policies/no-fear-act">

Label: No FEAR Act

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size because it is partially obscured (smallest space is 60.6px by 19.2px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 8.6px instead of at least 24px.

Element 7 HHS-4e6001ad

Element path: li > ul > li > a

Snippet:

<a href="/foia">

Label: FOIA

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (23.5px by 25px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 19.2px instead of at least 24px.

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order) HHS-08dfb35c

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Disabilities affected: ~109People who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~109 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 10,929 affected page loads) ~33People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. <h6 class="icon-email">

`[user-scalable="no"]` is used in the `<meta name="viewport">` element or the `[maximum-scale]` attribute is less than 5. (rule: meta-viewport) HHS-d8012402

Disabling zooming is problematic for users with low vision who rely on screen magnification to properly see the contents of a web page. Learn more about the viewport meta tag.

Disabilities affected: ~262People with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~262 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 10,929 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 HHS-f7deea39

Element path: head > meta

Snippet:

<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1,maximum-scale=1,minimum-scale=1,user-sc…">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • user-scalable=no on <meta> tag disables zooming on mobile devices
Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size) HHS-9bd59b3f

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Disabilities affected: ~240People with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~240 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 10,929 affected page loads) ~634People with limited reach, strength, or stamina. Label: Accessibility (Opens in new window)

  • Target has insufficient size (83.6px by 20px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 17.2px instead of at least 24px.

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (110.4px by 20px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 17.2px instead of at least 24px.

Accessibility Findings

https://direct.fda.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 84

Axe findings: 4

Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast) HHS-4c09f841

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Disabilities affected: ~160People with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~160 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 6,649 affected page loads) ~286People who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Estimated ~286 people potentially excluded (4.3% prevalence × 6,649 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 HHS-fff8fa8b

Element path: tbody > tr > td > input#P101_LOGIN

Snippet:

<input type="button" onclick="SubmitClick();" value="Login" class="btn-red disable-on-submit" style="width: 215px; min-width: 85%; margin-top: 28px; border-radius: 10px;" id="P101_LOGIN" autocomplete="off">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has insufficient color contrast of 4.04 (foreground color: #ffffff, background color: #da4f49, font size: 9.0pt (12px), font weight: bold). Expected contrast ratio of 4.5:1
Form elements do not have associated labels (rule: label) HHS-8021544f

Labels ensure that form controls are announced properly by assistive technologies, like screen readers. Learn more about form element labels.

Disabilities affected: ~66People who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~66 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 6,649 affected page loads) ~160People with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~160 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 6,649 affected page loads) ~20People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~20 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 6,649 affected page loads) ~146People with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~146 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 6,649 affected page loads)

Affected elements (2):

Element 1 HHS-15ba1d86

Element path: td.disclaimer > div#P101_AGREE > div.apex-item-option > input#P101_AGREE_0

Snippet:

<input type="checkbox" id="P101_AGREE_0" name="P101_AGREE" data-display="" value="Y" autocomplete="off">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Form element has explicit <label> that is hidden
  • Fix any of the following:
  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element has no placeholder attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

Element 2 HHS-13b027a6

Element path: tbody > tr > td > input#P_HP_NAME

Snippet:

<input type="text" id="P_HP_NAME" name="P_HP_NAME" class="text_field apex-item-text apex-hp" style="position: absolute !important; left: -99999px !important;" value="" size="30" autocomplete="off">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element has no placeholder attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
Some elements have a `[tabindex]` value greater than 0 (rule: tabindex) HHS-2f59774b

A value greater than 0 implies an explicit navigation ordering. Although technically valid, this often creates frustrating experiences for users who rely on assistive technologies. Learn more about the `tabindex` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~66People who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~66 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 6,649 affected page loads) ~20People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~146 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 6,649 affected page loads)

Accessibility Findings

https://contact.fda.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 94

Axe findings: 2

`button`, `link`, and `menuitem` elements do not have accessible names. (rule: aria-command-name) HHS-bc890b7a

When an element doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it with a generic name, making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. Learn how to make command elements more accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~5People who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~5 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 517 affected page loads) ~2People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~2 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 517 affected page loads) ~11People with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~11 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 517 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 HHS-b0fb7fa2

Element path: div.region > aside.lcds-card > ul.lcds-description-list > li.lcds-description-list__item

Snippet:

<li role="menuitem" class="lcds-description-list__item">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
Some elements have a `[tabindex]` value greater than 0 (rule: tabindex) HHS-c6fae3c6

A value greater than 0 implies an explicit navigation ordering. Although technically valid, this often creates frustrating experiences for users who rely on assistive technologies. Learn more about the `tabindex` attribute.

Label: Skip to main content

Download full axe findings JSON | Download full axe findings CSV

Accessibility Findings

https://labels.fda.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 65

Axe findings: 6

`<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute (rule: html-has-lang) HHS-2e5ce04d

If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. Learn more about the `lang` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~4People who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~4 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 412 affected page loads) ~1People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~1 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 412 affected page loads) ~19People with cognitive, learning, or language differences. Estimated ~19 people potentially excluded (4.7% prevalence × 412 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 HHS-699264ce

Element path: html

Snippet:

<html xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" xmlns:addthis="http://www.addthis.com/help/api-spec">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The <html> element does not have a lang attribute
Form elements do not have associated labels (rule: label) HHS-00bfc9dd

Labels ensure that form controls are announced properly by assistive technologies, like screen readers. Learn more about form element labels.

Disabilities affected: ~4People who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~9 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 412 affected page loads)

Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name) HHS-ae586f1f

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

  • Element has no title attribute

Element 1 HHS-8cc0e337

Element path: div.col-md-3 > ul > li > a

Snippet:

<a href="https://jobs.cdc.gov" data-aura-rendered-by="357:0">

Label: Jobs

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (27.5px by 15px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 22px instead of at least 24px.

Accessibility Findings

https://pids.cdc.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 63

Axe findings: 7

Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast) HHS-c57b96c9

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Disabilities affected: ~5People with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~5 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 220 affected page loads) ~9People who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Estimated ~9 people potentially excluded (4.3% prevalence × 220 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 HHS-02e4ed90

Element path: div#content-container > div#content > p#warning > font

Snippet:

<font color="red">

Label: Warning:

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has insufficient color contrast of 3.5 (foreground color: #ff0000, background color: #f0f0f0, font size: 10.5pt (14px), font weight: normal). Expected contrast ratio of 4.5:1
Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order) HHS-1f19addf

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Disabilities affected: ~2People who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~2 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 220 affected page loads) ~1People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~5 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 220 affected page loads)

Snippet:

<h6 class="hidden-four hidden-three">

Label: ABOUT

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Heading order invalid
Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt) HHS-88717810

Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. Learn more about the `alt` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~2People who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~2 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 220 affected page loads) ~1People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~1 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 220 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 HHS-f17cb64a

Element path: div#header-container > div#sams-logo-titlebar > a > img.samsheader

Snippet:

<img src="../images/SAMSHalfLogo.png" class="samsheader">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have an alt attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
Form elements do not have associated labels (rule: label) HHS-02ce293f

Labels ensure that form controls are announced properly by assistive technologies, like screen readers. Learn more about form element labels.

Disabilities affected: ~2People who are blind or have no functional vision.   • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element has no placeholder attribute

Element path: fieldset#gridcard-fieldset-visible-one > center#gridcard-include > form#igauthform > input.input-medium

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element has no placeholder attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name) HHS-6bdbb20d

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

Accessibility Findings

https://hivrisk.cdc.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 70

Axe findings: 8

Buttons do not have an accessible name (rule: button-name) HHS-2cada516

When a button doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it as "button", making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. Learn how to make buttons more accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~2People who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~2 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 180 affected page loads) ~1People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~1 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 180 affected page loads)

Affected elements (2):

Element 1 HHS-eda0b88b

Element path: div.col-lg-5 > div.header_imput_1 > div.col-lg-2 > button.w3-btn

Snippet:

<button type="submit" name="search_sbt" value="Search" class="w3-btn w3-blue w3-round-xlarge" tabindex="4">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have inner text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

Element 2 HHS-9a9cf881

Element path: div.o-widget-container > div.o-body > div.c-search > button#search__icon

Snippet:

<button id="search__icon" class="c-search__icon">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have inner text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

Element path: div.wpb_raw_code > div.wpb_wrapper > a > img.img-fluid

Snippet:

<img class="img-fluid" src="wp-content/themes/hrrt/img/HRRT-buttons-01.png">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have an alt attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name) HHS-c8e3b45b

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~2People who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~2 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 180 affected page loads) ~1People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~1 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 180 affected page loads) ~4People with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~4 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 180 affected page loads)

Element 1 HHS-bd3017c7

Element path: div.wpb_wrapper > div.wpb_raw_code > div.wpb_wrapper > a

Snippet:

<a href="https://hivrisk.cdc.gov/what-is-hiv">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Element is in tab order and does not have accessible text
  • Fix any of the following:
  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute

Lists do not contain only `<li>` elements and script supporting elements (`<script>` and `<template>`). (rule: list) HHS-5a55fe26

Screen readers have a specific way of announcing lists. Ensuring proper list structure aids screen reader output. Learn more about proper list structure.

Disabilities affected: ~2People who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~2 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 180 affected page loads) ~1People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~1 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 180 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 HHS-9d524d62

Element path: div.container > div.row > div.col-md-3 > ul.list-inline

Snippet:

<ul class="list-inline mt-3">

Label: Facebook Twitter Youtube Instagram

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • List element has direct children that are not allowed: br
Some elements have a `[tabindex]` value greater than 0 (rule: tabindex) HHS-24c04707

A value greater than 0 implies an explicit navigation ordering. Although technically valid, this often creates frustrating experiences for users who rely on assistive technologies. Learn more about the `tabindex` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~2People who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~2 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 180 affected page loads) ~1People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~4 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 180 affected page loads)

Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size) HHS-4a131f46

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Disabilities affected: ~4People with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~4 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 180 affected page loads) ~10People with limited reach, strength, or stamina. Estimated ~10 people potentially excluded (5.8% prevalence × 180 affected page loads)

Affected elements (7):

Element 1 HHS-7f3f0abd

Element path: div.o-widget-container > div.o-body > div.c-search > button#search__icon

Snippet:

<button id="search__icon" class="c-search__icon">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (22px by 22px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 22px instead of at least 24px.

Element path: div.col-md-3 > ul > li > a

Snippet:

<a href="https://jobs.cdc.gov/">

  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 23.4px instead of at least 24px.

Document does not have a main landmark. (rule: landmark-one-main) HHS-88fbf1d3

One main landmark helps screen reader users navigate a web page. Learn more about landmarks.

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 96

Axe findings: 2

Elements with an ARIA `[role]` that require children to contain a specific `[role]` are missing some or all of those required children. (rule: aria-required-children) HHS-62afd2c1

Some ARIA parent roles must contain specific child roles to perform their intended accessibility functions. Learn more about roles and required children elements.

Elements with visible text labels do not have matching accessible names. (rule: label-content-name-mismatch) HHS-8322287e

Visible text labels that do not match the accessible name can result in a confusing experience for screen reader users. Learn more about accessible names.

Download full axe findings JSON | Download full axe findings CSV

Accessibility Findings

https://www.access.fda.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 82

Axe findings: 4

Links rely on color to be distinguishable. (rule: link-in-text-block) HHS-c1cd6bd5

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Link text that is discernible improves the experience for users with low vision. Learn how to make links distinguishable.

Disabilities affected: ~4People with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~4 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 174 affected page loads) ~7People who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Estimated ~7 people potentially excluded (4.3% prevalence × 174 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 HHS-bc13f98b

Element path: div#help-desk > div.col-xs-4 > p > a

Snippet:

<a target="_blank" href="https://www.fda.gov/industry/electronic-submissions-gateway-next-generatio…">

Label: FDA Electronic Submissions Gateway Next Generation

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 1.88:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #005f9f, surrounding text: #333333)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text
Some elements have a `[tabindex]` value greater than 0 (rule: tabindex) HHS-1e5656df

A value greater than 0 implies an explicit navigation ordering. Although technically valid, this often creates frustrating experiences for users who rely on assistive technologies. Learn more about the `tabindex` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~2People who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~2 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 174 affected page loads) ~1People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~1 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 174 affected page loads) ~4People with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~4 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 174 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 HHS-4aa8865a

Element path: div.quicklinks > ul > li > a

Snippet:

<a href="#main" accesskey="2" tabindex="1">

Label: Skip to main page content

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has a tabindex greater than 0
Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size) HHS-1835aaf7

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Disabilities affected: ~4People with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~4 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 174 affected page loads) ~10People with limited reach, strength, or stamina. Estimated ~10 people potentially excluded (5.8% prevalence × 174 affected page loads)

Affected elements (13):

Element 1 HHS-8ca6ef45

Element path: div.col-xs-6 > ul.additionalInfo > li > a

Snippet:

<a target="_blank" href="https://www.fda.gov/cosmetics/voluntary-cosmetic-registration-program">

Label: Voluntary Cosmetic Registration Program

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (256.8px by 16px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 22.6px instead of at least 24px.

Document does not have a main landmark. (rule: landmark-one-main) HHS-20b41120

One main landmark helps screen reader users navigate a web page. Learn more about landmarks.

Disabilities affected: ~2People who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~2 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 174 affected page loads) ~1People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~1 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 174 affected page loads) ~4People with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~4 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 174 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 HHS-8c80af08

Element path: html.js

Snippet:

<html lang="en" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" style="" class=" js flexbox canvas canvastext webgl touch geolocation postmessage no-websq…">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Document does not have a main landmark

Accessibility Findings

https://cdrh-rst.fda.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 92

Axe findings: 3

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order) HHS-74b4de02

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Disabilities affected: ~2People who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~2 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 170 affected page loads) ~1People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Element path: div#root > div > div#skipmenu > a.skippy

Snippet:

<a class="skippy sr-only-focusable" href="#tabContent">

Label: Skip directly to site content

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • No skip link target

Element 2 HHS-a7d7ee6f

Element path: div#root > div > div#skipmenu > a.skippy

Snippet:

<a class="skippy sr-only-focusable" href="#tabs-nav">

Label: Skip directly to page options

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • No skip link target
Document does not have a main landmark. (rule: landmark-one-main) HHS-676d59b9

One main landmark helps screen reader users navigate a web page. Learn more about landmarks.

Disabilities affected: ~2People who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~2 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 169 affected page loads) ~1People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~4 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 169 affected page loads)

Element 1 HHS-c50de867

Download full axe findings JSON | Download full axe findings CSV

Accessibility Findings

https://www.accessdata.fda.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 50

Axe findings: 3

Document doesn't have a `<title>` element (rule: document-title) HHS-75cc9990

The title gives screen reader users an overview of the page, and search engine users rely on it heavily to determine if a page is relevant to their search. Learn more about document titles.

Disabilities affected: ~2People who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~2 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 167 affected page loads) ~1People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~1 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 167 affected page loads) ~4People with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~4 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 167 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 HHS-3e034b55

Element path: html

Snippet:

<html>

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Document does not have a non-empty <title> element
`<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute (rule: html-has-lang) HHS-da98b139

If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. Learn more about the `lang` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~2People who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~2 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 167 affected page loads) ~1People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~1 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 167 affected page loads) ~8People with cognitive, learning, or language differences. Estimated ~8 people potentially excluded (4.7% prevalence × 167 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 HHS-f97ee020

Element path: html

Snippet:

<html>

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The <html> element does not have a lang attribute

Element 1 HHS-39d76798

Element path: div > fieldset > p.alert > a

Snippet:

<a target="_blank" href="https://www.cdc.gov/wtc/pdfs/applicationForms/WTCHP-OnlineSignatureForm-Pr…">

Label: Download signature form

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has insufficient color contrast of 3.59 (foreground color: #007bff, background color: #fff3cd, font size: 12.0pt (16px), font weight: normal). Expected contrast ratio of 4.5:1

Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt) HHS-02802166

Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. Learn more about the `alt` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~1People who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 104 affected page loads) ~0People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~0 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 104 affected page loads)

Affected elements (4):

Element 1 HHS-93d790d5

Element path: div.col-lg-6 > div.col-lg-12 > a#cphBody_hlFDNYResponder > img#cphBody_imgFDNYRespoderLogo

Snippet:

<img id="cphBody_imgFDNYRespoderLogo" src="App_Themes/Oasis_Bootstrap/Images/FDNY_Logo2.svg">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have an alt attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

Links rely on color to be distinguishable. (rule: link-in-text-block) HHS-f39c1987

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Link text that is discernible improves the experience for users with low vision. Learn how to make links distinguishable.

Disabilities affected: ~2People with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~2 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 104 affected page loads) ~4People who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Estimated ~4 people potentially excluded (4.3% prevalence × 104 affected page loads)

Affected elements (5):

Element 1 HHS-2cdc9a09

Element path: div.row > div.col-lg-6 > div.col-lg-12 > a#cphBody_hlGeneralResponderDetails

Snippet:

<a id="cphBody_hlGeneralResponderDetails" class="aspHyperLink" href="GeneralResponder" target="_self">

Label: General Responder

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 1.21:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #1875bd, surrounding text: #646464)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text

  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text

Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name) HHS-c0f1be32

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

https://mvps.cdc.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 70

Axe findings: 6

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order) HHS-5c4d3e2e

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Disabilities affected: ~1People who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 81 affected page loads) ~0People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~0 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 81 affected page loads) ~2People with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~2 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 81 affected page loads)

Snippet:

  • Heading order invalid
Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt) HHS-a55f4b60

Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. Learn more about the `alt` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~1People who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 81 affected page loads) ~0People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~0 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 81 affected page loads)

Element path: div#header-container > div.titlebar > a > img.samsheader

Snippet:

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have an alt attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
Form elements do not have associated labels (rule: label) HHS-849a2c5f

Labels ensure that form controls are announced properly by assistive technologies, like screen readers. Learn more about form element labels.

Disabilities affected: ~1People who are blind or have no functional vision.   • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 HHS-2de28b52

Element path: header#header > div#header-container > div.titlebar > a

Snippet:

<a href="https://sams.cdc.gov">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Element is in tab order and does not have accessible text
  • Fix any of the following:
  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
Skip links are not focusable. (rule: skip-link) HHS-035c3dee

Including a skip link can help users skip to the main content to save time. Learn more about skip links.

Element path: body > div#skipmenu > a.skippy

Snippet:

<a href="#share-bar" class="skippy">

Label: Skip directly to page options

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • No skip link target

Accessibility Findings

https://fsdx.fda.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 91

Axe findings: 3

Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast) HHS-84a81abd

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Disabilities affected: ~2People with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~2 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 77 affected page loads) ~3People who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Estimated ~3 people potentially excluded (4.3% prevalence × 77 affected page loads)

Affected elements (15):

Element 1 HHS-9f55b5e4

Element path: div.oramission > div.missionContent > p > a

Snippet:

<a target="_blank" href="https://www.pfp-ifss.org/">

Label: Partnership for Food Protection (PFP)

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has insufficient color contrast of 3.63 (foreground color: #428bca, background color: #ffffff, font size: 12.0pt (16px), font weight: normal). Expected contrast ratio of 4.5:1

Links rely on color to be distinguishable. (rule: link-in-text-block) HHS-d27b2c41

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Link text that is discernible improves the experience for users with low vision. Learn how to make links distinguishable.

Disabilities affected: ~2People with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~2 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 77 affected page loads) ~3People who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Estimated ~3 people potentially excluded (4.3% prevalence × 77 affected page loads)

Affected elements (4):

Element 1 HHS-9d809a9f

Element path: div.oramission > div.missionContent > p > a

Snippet:

<a target="_blank" href="https://www.pfp-ifss.org/">

Label: Partnership for Food Protection (PFP)

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 2.11:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #428bca, surrounding text: #455372)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text

Element path: div.oramission > div.missionContent > p > a

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 2.11:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #428bca, surrounding text: #455372)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order) HHS-5e533dbd

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Disabilities affected: ~1People who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 68 affected page loads) ~0People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~0 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 68 affected page loads) ~1People with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~1 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 68 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 HHS-fd8f2c52

Element path: span.ng-star-inserted > div.alert > form.ng-untouched > h5.text-fdablack

Snippet:

<h5 class="text-fdablack">

Label: FURLS User Registration

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Heading order invalid
Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt) HHS-88f4e391

Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. Learn more about the `alt` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~1People who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 68 affected page loads) ~0People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~0 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 68 affected page loads)

Affected elements (2):

Element 1 HHS-3df3ab6d

Element path: div.row > div.col-lg-6 > div.d-flex > img.img-thumbnail

Snippet:

<img width="190" src="./assets/images/COSM_SubmissionHelpGuide.png" class="img-thumbnail" style="cursor: pointer;">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have an alt attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

Element 2 HHS-3df3ab6d

Element path: div.row > div.col-lg-6 > div.d-flex > img.img-thumbnail

Snippet:

<img width="190" src="./assets/images/COSM_SubmissionHelpVideo.png" class="img-thumbnail" style="cursor: pointer;">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have an alt attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
